disclaimer: This is an informational comparison of published documents, not legal advice. It describes what public documents say, with citations. Provider documents change and automated extraction can be wrong or stale — always review the current source documents yourself.

dimensions.label: Availability definition
dimensions.guidance: How uptime/availability is defined and the commitment percentage(s). Categorize the basis as one of: instance-level, region-level, cluster/throughput-based, API-error-rate-based, or other. Include the commitment percentage(s) (e.g. 99.99% region, 99.5% instance). If the provider publishes no uptime commitment, value = 'no SLA / uptime not committed'.

dimensions.label: Credit regime
dimensions.guidance: Service-credit structure: the tier mapping (uptime threshold -> credit percentage), the maximum credit, and whether credits are stated to be the sole and exclusive remedy. Give the tiers compactly.

dimensions.label: Claim mechanics
dimensions.guidance: Whether the customer must file a claim to receive credits, the claim window and how it is measured (e.g. within 30 days / end of billing cycle), and what evidence the customer must supply.

dimensions.label: SLA exclusions
dimensions.guidance: Notable exclusions from the SLA as a short categorical checklist plus a brief note. Look for: force majeure, customer fault/misuse, scheduled or emergency maintenance, beta/preview services, single-AZ deployments, throttling, external network/ISP issues. List the ones present.

dimensions.label: Capacity & reservation terms
dimensions.guidance: Existence and nature of reserved or committed-capacity terms in the PUBLIC documents: reserved instances, committed-use/savings plans, take-or-pay, use-it-or-lose-it, cancellation rights. Where the meaningful commitment terms are negotiated privately (enterprise commitment vehicles), say so explicitly with 'negotiated, not published'. Do not infer terms that are not in the documents.

dimensions.label: Data use & AI training
dimensions.guidance: What the terms say about the provider's rights to use customer data, and specifically any language about training or improving the provider's AI/ML models on customer content. State the default position (e.g. 'does not train on customer data' vs. 'may use to improve services') and any opt-in/opt-out.

dimensions.label: Suspension rights
dimensions.guidance: The grounds on which the provider may suspend the service (e.g. AUP breach, non-payment, security risk, legal process) and whether prior notice is required or suspension can be immediate.

dimensions.label: Termination
dimensions.guidance: Termination for convenience rights (which side, and any notice period), and the effect on data retrieval after termination — the post-termination window during which the customer can still retrieve data.

dimensions.label: Unilateral modification
dimensions.guidance: Whether and how the provider may unilaterally change the terms, and any notice commitment before changes take effect (e.g. 30/90 days' notice, or 'effective on posting').

dimensions.label: Governing law & disputes
dimensions.guidance: Governing law, the forum/venue, whether disputes go to binding arbitration (yes/no), and whether there is a class-action waiver (yes/no). Keep each element short.

documents.name: SLA for Azure Virtual Machines (compute & GPU)
Microsoft Product Terms for Online Services
Google Cloud Platform Terms of Service
Google Cloud Service Specific Terms (AI/ML data use)
Google Cloud Platform Acceptable Use Policy
Crusoe Legal Center (Cloud Platform Terms of Service)

availability_definition.value: instance-level and region-level; 99.99% region (multi-AZ), 99.5% single instance; 'Unavailable' = no external connectivity
availability_definition.citation: AWS makes two SLA commitments for Amazon EC2

credit_regime.citation: this SLA sets forth your sole and exclusive remedies

claim_mechanics.value: Must file claim via AWS Support Center; by end of second billing cycle after incident; must include dates/times, resource IDs, request logs corroborating outage

sla_exclusions.value: force majeure/beyond demarcation point; customer actions/inactions; customer equipment/software; suspension/termination per Agreement; beta services excluded (Service Terms 2.2)

capacity_reservation.value: Reserved Instances, Savings Plans, Dedicated Host Reservations, Capacity Blocks for ML: noncancellable, nonrefundable, nontransferable; pro rata refund only if AWS terminates without cause
capacity_reservation.citation: Savings Plans, EC2 Reserved Instances...are noncancellable

commitment_program.note: AWS's enterprise pricing page describes custom, negotiated volume commitments; the actual EDP discount rates and terms are confidential and not published.

data_use_ai_training.value: Default: will not access/use Your Content except to provide Services or comply with law; may use interaction info to improve Services; AI Services may use AI Content to improve models with AWS Organizations opt-out
data_use_ai_training.citation: We will not access or use Your Content except as necessary

suspension_rights.value: Grounds: security risk, adverse system impact, liability, fraud, material breach, non-payment, insolvency; suspension immediate upon notice
suspension_rights.citation: We may suspend...immediately upon notice to you

termination.value: Customer terminates for convenience anytime by closing account; AWS for convenience on 30 days' notice; 30-day post-termination data retrieval window (if fees paid)
termination.citation: Termination for Convenience; during the 30 days following the Termination Date

unilateral_modification.value: AWS may modify Agreement anytime by posting revised version; effective upon posting or as stated in email; 90 days' notice for adverse SLA changes

governing_law_disputes.value: Governing law and forum vary by AWS Contracting Party. For Amazon Web Services, Inc. (US): Washington State law, King County, WA courts. Disputes are subject to binding arbitration (AAA commercial rules) and a class-action waiver (individual basis only).
governing_law_disputes.citation: any dispute resolution proceedings will be conducted only on an individual basis
governing_law_disputes.override_note: Verified by counsel 2026-07-14 against the AWS Customer Agreement, Section 12.

availability_definition.value: not specified in provided text; the actual SLA percentages/definitions are only in downloadable SLA PDFs, not in the page content provided

credit_regime.value: not specified in provided text; SLA describes 'Microsoft's commitments for uptime' but tier/credit details are in the downloadable PDF, not shown. Customer Agreement states SLA remedies are Customer's remedies for breach of Online Services warranty
credit_regime.citation: Customer's remedies for breach of this warranty are described in the SLA

claim_mechanics.value: not specified in provided text; claim filing procedure and window are in the downloadable SLA PDF, not shown

sla_exclusions.value: Beta/preview services excluded ('Previews are not included in the SLA'). Full SLA exclusion list is in downloadable PDF, not shown. Present: beta/preview; throttling referenced ('temporary throttling') though not framed as SLA exclusion
sla_exclusions.citation: Previews are not included in the SLA for the corresponding Online Service

source.name: Microsoft Product Terms for Online Services

capacity_reservation.value: Consumption services may be sold as prepayments, reservations, or other subscriptions (Azure reservations); prices subject to change. Detailed commitment/cancellation terms largely negotiated, not published in these docs
capacity_reservation.citation: Consumption services may be sold as prepayments, reservations or other subscriptions

source.name: Microsoft Product Terms for Online Services

commitment_program.note: Microsoft's own docs describe the MACC as a contractual spend commitment associated with a negotiated agreement; the commitment terms themselves are not published.

data_use_ai_training.value: Does not train on customer data by default: 'Microsoft Generative AI Services will not use Customer Data to train any generative AI foundation model, except pursuant to Customer's documented instructions.' Output Content is Customer Data
data_use_ai_training.citation: will not use Customer Data to train any generative AI foundation model

source.name: Microsoft Product Terms for Online Services

suspension_rights.value: Grounds: AUP violation, non-payment/declined charge, material breach, security risk, trade-law risk. Prior notice generally required (30 days for breach; reasonable notice for AUP) but immediate suspension allowed to prevent unauthorized data access or protect systems
suspension_rights.citation: Microsoft reasonably believes immediate suspension is required to prevent unauthorized access

termination.value: Termination for convenience: either party on 60 days' notice. Post-termination data retrieval: for non-renewed/disabled subscriptions, Customer has 90 days to extract Customer Data
termination.citation: Either party may terminate this Agreement without cause on 60 days' notice

unilateral_modification.value: Microsoft may update the Agreement. For General Terms/other terms, 60 days' notice before changes take effect (effective on renewal). Product Terms: material adverse changes apply at renewal, others on publication. SLA/DPA changes per those documents. AUP may change at any time without notice
unilateral_modification.citation: Customer will be notified at least 60 days before changes take effect to these General Terms

governing_law_disputes.value: Governing law: State of Washington and US federal law. Venue: King County, WA for actions against Microsoft (non-Europe); Ireland for European affiliates; Customer HQ if Microsoft sues. Arbitration: no (exclusive court venues). Class-action waiver: not specified
governing_law_disputes.citation: governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Washington

availability_definition.value: Instance-level and multi-zone availability, measured as Monthly Uptime Percentage (minutes-based). Commitments: Instances in Multiple Zones >=99.99% (Premium), Load balancing >=99.99%, Single Instance Memory Optimized >=99.95%, Single Instance other families >=99.9%; Standard Tier >=99.9%; lower tiers (99.95%) for Mexico/Stockholm regions.
availability_definition.citation: Monthly Uptime Percentage ... Instances in Multiple Zones >= 99.99%

credit_regime.value: Tiered credit of % of monthly bill for the non-compliant Covered Service. Multi-Zone/LB (Premium): 99.00%-<99.99% =10%, 95.00%-<99.00% =25%, <95.00% =100%. Single Instance: 95.00%-<99.9x% =10%, 90.00%-<95.00% =25%, <90.00% =100%. Sole and exclusive remedy stated.
credit_regime.citation: This SLA states Customer's sole and exclusive remedy

claim_mechanics.value: Customer must file a claim: notify Google technical support within 60 days from becoming eligible, and provide log files showing Downtime Periods and dates/times. Failure to comply forfeits credit.
claim_mechanics.citation: must notify Google technical support within 60 days

sla_exclusions.value: Present: pre-GA/beta features; features excluded in Documentation; force majeure (factors outside Google's reasonable control); customer or third-party software/hardware; abuse/Agreement violations; quota-based errors. Also Access Approval disruptions; Pre-GA offerings not covered.
sla_exclusions.citation: SLA Exclusions ... caused by factors outside of Google's reasonable control

capacity_reservation.value: Public terms include Committed Units (non-cancellable, take-or-pay: pay whether or not used) with auto-renewal, no resell/transfer; Compute Engine future reservations (Google approves in its discretion). Refund of unused prepaid Fees only if Google non-renews/terminates or discontinues. Specific commitment amounts negotiated, not published.
capacity_reservation.citation: Customer will pay the Fees for those Committed Units ... whether or not they are used

source.name: Google Cloud Platform Terms of Service

commitment_program.note: Google publishes the mechanics of committed-use discounts for Compute Engine; specific negotiated enterprise commitment terms are not published.

data_use_ai_training.value: Default: does not train on customer data. 'Google will not use Customer Data to train or fine-tune any AI/ML models without Customer's prior permission or instruction.' Generally Google only processes Customer Data per the Cloud Data Processing Addendum. Opt-in via customer permission/instruction.
data_use_ai_training.citation: Google will not use Customer Data to train or fine-tune any AI/ML models

source.name: Google Cloud Service Specific Terms (AI/ML data use)

suspension_rights.value: Grounds: AUP violation (notice + 24 hrs to cure before suspension); non-payment/late payment; protecting Services/infrastructure/other customers; suspected unauthorized access; legal compliance; breach of Restrictions/Service Specific Terms. Immediate suspension allowed for the 'Other Suspension' grounds; AUP violations get notice and 24-hour cure.
suspension_rights.citation: Google may immediately Suspend all or part of Customer's use

source.name: Google Cloud Platform Terms of Service

termination.value: Customer may terminate for convenience anytime on prior written notice (subject to financial commitments). Google may terminate GCP Services for convenience on 30 days' prior written notice. On termination, access to Services and Customer Data terminates unless otherwise stated; no general post-termination data retrieval window (EU Data Act adds a Data Recovery Period of >=30 days where applicable).
termination.citation: Google may terminate this Agreement ... with 30 days' prior written notice

source.name: Google Cloud Platform Terms of Service

unilateral_modification.value: Google may update the Agreement; material updates to GCP terms become effective 30 days after posting (immediately if for new functionality or required by law). Service discontinuation: at least 12 months' notice (with exceptions). URL Terms/SLA updates: material updates effective 30 days after posting.
unilateral_modification.citation: material updates to this Agreement will become effective 30 days after they are posted

source.name: Google Cloud Platform Terms of Service

governing_law_disputes.value: Governing law: California (excluding conflict rules); venue: state/federal courts of Santa Clara County, California. Binding arbitration: no (litigation in courts). Class-action waiver: not specified. (Different rules for US government entities.)
governing_law_disputes.citation: GOVERNED BY CALIFORNIA LAW ... LITIGATED EXCLUSIVELY IN ... SANTA CLARA COUNTY

source.name: Google Cloud Platform Terms of Service

availability_definition.value: API-error-rate-based: Storage Monthly Uptime Percentage >= 99.9% for AI Object Storage (cwobject.com endpoint); computed as 100% minus average Error Percentage per 5-minute interval (InternalError/ServiceUnavailable errors / total requests). Applies only to Object Storage, not compute.

credit_regime.value: Tiers (% of monthly bill for affected AZ credited to future bills): 99%-99.9% -> 10%; 95%-98.999% -> 25%; <94.999% -> 100%. Credits are future-use monetary credits only, no refund. Stated as sole and exclusive remedy.
credit_regime.citation: sole and exclusive remedy for any failure

claim_mechanics.value: Customer must request credit: notify support@coreweave.com within 30 days of becoming eligible; must provide applicable AZ and request logs documenting dates/times of each Error. Non-compliance forfeits the credit.
claim_mechanics.citation: must notify CoreWeave technical support ... within thirty (30) days

sla_exclusions.value: Present: force majeure / factors outside CoreWeave's reasonable control (incl. internet beyond demarcation point); customer fault/misuse (customer actions/inactions, customer/third-party software/hardware); TOS/AUP violations & suspension/termination; throttling (system quotas); scheduled maintenance (in-advance communicated); beta/pre-GA features.
sla_exclusions.citation: Exclusions. The Storage Financial Credits do not apply

capacity_reservation.value: not specified in the provided public documents (no reserved instance, committed-use, take-or-pay, or cancellation terms appear)

data_use_ai_training.value: not specified; provided documents (Terms of Use, AUP, Storage SLA) contain no language on provider use of customer data for training/improving AI/ML models (governed by TOS/Privacy Policy not included here)

suspension_rights.value: Under AUP, CoreWeave may suspend or terminate use of Services if it determines in good faith the Customer violated the AUP; may also intercept/block content or traffic where used unlawfully. Applies even if violation unintentional/unauthorized; exercisable at any time in sole discretion (no prior-notice requirement stated). Terms of Use: payment method problems may suspend/cancel a Transaction.
suspension_rights.citation: suspend or terminate Customer's use ... applies even if a violation is committed unintentionally

termination.value: Termination for convenience and post-termination data retrieval window not specified in the provided documents. Terms of Use: right to use CoreWeave Sites ceases immediately upon breach; AUP allows CoreWeave to terminate for violations. No customer termination-for-convenience or data-retrieval period stated.
termination.citation: your right to use the CoreWeave Sites will cease immediately

unilateral_modification.value: Terms of Use: Company may update Terms in its sole discretion; 'All changes are effective immediately when we post them.' AUP: 'CoreWeave may modify this AUP at any time by posting a revised version.' No advance-notice commitment; effective on posting.
unilateral_modification.citation: All changes are effective immediately when we post them

governing_law_disputes.value: Governing law: New York (Terms of Use). Forum/venue: courts in New York, New York. Arbitration: optional at Company's sole discretion (binding, AAA rules, NY law) - not mandatory. Class-action waiver: yes (disputes resolved individually, no class action).
governing_law_disputes.citation: resolved individually, without resort to any form of class action

availability_definition.value: no SLA / uptime not committed; Services provided 'AS-IS', no warranty of uninterrupted service

credit_regime.value: No service-level uptime credit regime. Only discretionary credits (coupons, promos, prepaid, fee adjustments) expiring 12 months from issuance; no tiers, no sole-remedy language for uptime.
credit_regime.citation: Lambda may, at its sole discretion, choose to offer credits

claim_mechanics.value: not specified (no SLA credit claim process; credits are discretionary and not claim-based)

sla_exclusions.value: No SLA exists. Related risk-shifting present: force majeure; customer fault/misuse; scheduled/emergency maintenance ('planned downtime for upgrades and maintenance'); beta/preview services; external network/ISP issues; throttling.
sla_exclusions.citation: temporarily suspend access to the Services during planned downtime for upgrades and maintenance

capacity_reservation.value: Public terms reference only Orders and published pricing; no reserved-instance, committed-use, take-or-pay or cancellation terms published. Meaningful commitment terms are negotiated, not published (per-Order).
capacity_reservation.citation: Each Order for the Services will describe additional mutually agreed-upon limitations

data_use_ai_training.value: No explicit AI/ML training statement. Provider may process Customer Property to provide Services and may use aggregated/anonymized 'Usage Information' to improve Services; no opt-out stated. Default: may use anonymized/aggregated data to improve services.
data_use_ai_training.citation: use data ... in an aggregated and anonymous manner ... improving the Services

suspension_rights.value: Grounds: breach of Agreement/AUP, non-payment, excessive utilization affecting availability, suspected unauthorized access, legal/court/regulatory requirement. Suspension may be immediate ('immediately suspend, throttle or terminate').
suspension_rights.citation: Lambda may immediately suspend, throttle or terminate access to the Services

termination.value: Termination for convenience: either party may terminate when no Orders in effect; for-cause with 30-day cure. Post-termination data retrieval: no window guaranteed — Lambda may delete Customer Property upon termination without liability.
termination.citation: Lambda may ... delete the Customer Property in its possession without liability

unilateral_modification.value: Lambda may update Agreement unilaterally; updated version binding at start of next billing cycle. (Website Terms: changes effective 10 days after posting.)
unilateral_modification.citation: updated version of this Agreement shall be binding upon the Parties at the start of the next billing cycle

governing_law_disputes.value: Governing law: California. Forum if not arbitrated: N.D. Cal. or Santa Clara County state court. Binding arbitration: yes (AAA, FAA; 30-day opt-out). Class-action waiver: yes.
governing_law_disputes.citation: The Parties agree to arbitrate all claims ... waiving ... class or representative action

availability_definition.value: instance-level (per single GPU-enabled VM) and per-volume/per-resource. Commitments: 99.5% GPU VM, 99.5% Persistent Disks, 99.5% Shared Disks, 99.9% Container Registry, 99.9% Managed Kubernetes control plane. Measured as Monthly Uptime Percentage.

source.name: Crusoe Legal Center (Cloud Platform Terms of Service)

credit_regime.value: Tiered credit of monthly bill for the affected Covered Service. GPU VM/Persistent/Shared Disks: 95-99.5%=10%, 90-95%=25%, <90%=100%. Container Registry/Kubernetes: 99-99.9%=10%, 95-99%=25%, <95%=100%. Max credit capped at amount due for affected Covered Service that month. Stated as sole and exclusive remedy.
credit_regime.citation: This SLA states Customer's sole and exclusive remedy

source.name: Crusoe Legal Center (Cloud Platform Terms of Service)

claim_mechanics.value: Customer must request. Must notify Crusoe within 30 days from becoming eligible, and provide log files or monitoring data showing loss of connectivity with date and time of errors. Failure to comply forfeits the credit.
claim_mechanics.citation: Customer must notify Crusoe within 30 days

source.name: Crusoe Legal Center (Cloud Platform Terms of Service)

sla_exclusions.value: Present: force majeure (factors outside Crusoe's reasonable control); customer fault/third-party hardware/software; scheduled or emergency maintenance; beta/preview (pre-general availability features); AUP/agreement violations; quotas/throttling (quotas applied by system). Not addressed: single-AZ, external ISP.

source.name: Crusoe Legal Center (Cloud Platform Terms of Service)

capacity_reservation.value: Not specified in public documents; no reserved instances, committed-use, savings plans, take-or-pay or cancellation terms appear. Pricing incorporated by reference to external URL. Enterprise commitment terms appear negotiated, not published.
capacity_reservation.citation: The Fees for each Service are stated at https://crusoe.ai/cloud

source.name: Crusoe Legal Center (Cloud Platform Terms of Service)

data_use_ai_training.value: Does not use customer data for other purposes. Crusoe accesses/uses Customer Data only to provide the Services and TSS or as instructed, and will not use it for any other Crusoe products, services, or advertising. No AI/ML training language; no opt-in/opt-out stated.
data_use_ai_training.citation: will not use it for any other Crusoe products, services, or advertising

source.name: Crusoe Legal Center (Cloud Platform Terms of Service)

suspension_rights.value: AUP violations: notice + 24-hour cure before suspension. Other grounds (adverse impact on Services/network, suspected unauthorized access, legal compliance, breach of Restrictions/Service Specific Terms): immediate suspension. Non-payment: may suspend if overdue. Notice of basis given at Customer's request as soon as reasonably possible.
suspension_rights.citation: Crusoe may immediately Suspend all or part of Customer's use

source.name: Crusoe Legal Center (Cloud Platform Terms of Service)

termination.value: Termination for convenience: Customer any time on prior written notice; Crusoe any time with 30 days' prior written notice. On termination all rights and access to Services terminate including access to Customer Data; no defined post-termination data-retrieval window (data export must occur during the Term).
termination.citation: Crusoe may terminate this Agreement for its convenience... with 30 days' prior written notice

source.name: Crusoe Legal Center (Cloud Platform Terms of Service)

unilateral_modification.value: Crusoe may change the Agreement/URL Terms and pricing. Material changes effective 30 days after posting; changes for new functionality, DPST, or legally required are effective immediately. At least 90 days' advance notice for materially adverse SLA changes. Continued use = consent.
unilateral_modification.citation: material changes to the Agreement will become effective 30 days after they are posted

source.name: Crusoe Legal Center (Cloud Platform Terms of Service)

governing_law_disputes.value: Governing law: New York law (excluding conflict rules). Forum: exclusive federal/state courts of Denver County, Colorado. Arbitration: no (litigation). Class-action waiver: not specified.
governing_law_disputes.citation: GOVERNED BY NEW YORK LAW... LITIGATED EXCLUSIVELY IN THE... COURTS OF DENVER COUNTY, COLORADO

source.name: Crusoe Legal Center (Cloud Platform Terms of Service)

availability_definition.value: no SLA / uptime not committed. For Community Cloud, expressly no uptime warranty; Service provided 'as-is and as-available'.
availability_definition.citation: Runpod does not make any specific uptime warranties with respect to the Community Cloud Offerings

credit_regime.value: not specified; no service-credit structure in the document. Liability capped at lesser of 6-month fees paid or $100.
credit_regime.citation: LIMITED TO THE LESSER OF THE AMOUNT PAID... OR $100.00

claim_mechanics.value: not specified; no SLA credit claim process. Billing disputes directed to help@runpod.io.
claim_mechanics.citation: Please contact help@runpod.io regarding any billing disputes

sla_exclusions.value: No formal SLA. Related disclaimers present: force majeure ('cause beyond our reasonable control'); customer fault/misuse; interruption/cessation of transmission; Community/beta-type offerings disclaimed.
sla_exclusions.citation: any cause beyond our reasonable control

capacity_reservation.value: Subscriptions auto-renew for same-duration terms; no prorated refund on cancellation (use-it-or-lose-it flavor). No published reserved-instance/committed-use terms; enterprise commitment terms negotiated, not published.
capacity_reservation.citation: you will not be eligible for a prorated refund

data_use_ai_training.value: May access/use Your Content to provide Service, and use it in aggregated and anonymized form to update and improve Service and Runpod's products. No explicit AI/ML model-training statement; no opt-out stated.
data_use_ai_training.citation: use Your Content in an aggregated and anonymized form to update and improve the Service

suspension_rights.value: May suspend/terminate immediately and without notice for material breach, undue system stress, or legal requirement; non-payment may lead to suspension. Grounds: AUP breach, non-payment, legal process, security/harm.
suspension_rights.citation: immediately and without notice, suspend or terminate any Service

termination.value: Runpod may terminate for convenience at any time upon notice; customer may terminate by notice and closing Account. No post-termination data-retrieval window specified; Runpod may delete account/content at any time.
termination.citation: terminate these Terms of Use or your access to the Service at any time without cause upon notice

unilateral_modification.value: May modify Terms at sole discretion; notice given only by updating 'Last Updated' date; changes effective on posting via continued use. No fixed advance-notice period (pricing changes get 'reasonable advance notification').
unilateral_modification.citation: we will alert you about any changes by updating the “Last Updated” date

governing_law_disputes.value: Governing law: Delaware. Venue: NJ courts (Burlington County for severed/void-arbitration matters). Binding arbitration: yes (NAM, opt-out within 30 days). Class-action waiver: yes.
governing_law_disputes.citation: governed by... the laws of the State of Delaware; MANDATORY BINDING ARBITRATION AND A CLASS ACTION WAIVER

availability_definition.value: no SLA / uptime not committed. Terms state Company cannot guarantee availability and disclaims liability for downtime.
availability_definition.citation: Company cannot guarantee the Website and that Company Services will be always available

credit_regime.value: not specified; no service-credit structure. Payments are final and no refunds; refunds on unspent credits at sole discretion.
credit_regime.citation: All payments for credits spent on or through the Website are final and no refunds shall be issued

claim_mechanics.value: not specified. No SLA claim process; note a 1-year limitation period applies to any claim/cause of action.
claim_mechanics.citation: ANY CLAIM OR CAUSE OF ACTION ... MUST BE FILED WITHIN ONE (1) YEAR

sla_exclusions.value: No SLA exists. Related disclaimers present: force majeure (cause beyond reasonable control) and maintenance/interruptions; no service-level exclusions listed.
sla_exclusions.citation: may need to perform maintenance ... resulting in interruptions, delays, or errors

capacity_reservation.value: not specified. Terms describe on-demand rental of hosting from independent providers and purchase of credits; no reserved/committed-use terms published.
capacity_reservation.citation: Rental by users of hosting services ... from independent providers

data_use_ai_training.value: not specified re AI training. Governed by Privacy Policy/DPA; Feedback is assigned to Company for any use. Data-use rights not detailed in these terms.
data_use_ai_training.citation: you hereby assign to us ... any ideas, know-how, concepts, techniques ... in the Feedback

suspension_rights.value: Grounds: non-payment/chargebacks, false info, export-law violation, AUP/breach, or any/no reason; may be immediate and without notice.
suspension_rights.citation: DENY ACCESS ... WITHOUT NOTICE OR LIABILITY ... FOR ANY REASON OR FOR NO REASON

termination.value: Either side may terminate at any time for any reason; Company may terminate without notice and delete content. No post-termination data retrieval window; Company has no obligation to retain data.
termination.citation: Company has no obligation to retain a record of your account or any data

unilateral_modification.value: Company may modify at any time; changes effective on posting; user waives right to specific notice; continued use = acceptance.
unilateral_modification.citation: Modifications to this Agreement shall be effective after posting.

governing_law_disputes.value: Governing law: California. Forum: JAMS arbitration, Los Angeles, CA. Binding arbitration: yes. Class-action waiver: yes.
governing_law_disputes.citation: governed by the laws of California ... submitted to binding arbitration with ... JAMS
